item.text: The audit target is currently uncommitted and sits in a heavily dirty worktree, including modifications to `p3s_manifest_summary.py` and untracked standard P4 scripts/artifacts. I’ll treat the filesystem as the “actual current implementation,” while separately flagging durability/commit-gate consequences; I will not touch any of these changes.

    38	## Resume-skip validates existence, not completeness (found 2026-07-17, 4D corrected uthrow)
    39	`[[ -s $OUT ]] && skip` resume guards (used across many sbatch launchers) treat any non-empty
    40	output as done. Producers that atomic-save per-unit into one file (e.g. `unified_throw_cov.py
    41	do_throws` per-throw saves) leave VALID-but-partial files when interrupted — the resume then
    42	permanently skips them. Bit us: comb4dCc 55971617 failed on 15/160 missing throws because slabs
    43	31,34-39 were partial leftovers of an interrupted multinode run; all 40 array tasks "COMPLETED".
    44	Caught only by the combine's `--expected-throws` manifest gate. Repair: partials moved to
    45	`uq_4d/corrected/uthrow_slabs_4d/partial_20260716_interrupted/`, regen 56025478 -> combine
    46	56025481 -> adopt 56025483. Fix pattern for future launchers: content-validated resume (open the
    47	file, check unit inventory) or write-to-temp + rename-on-complete. (BEN-023.)

    66	**Headline numbers (Phase 18.2 MEFHC, 5-iter production):**
    67	- σ_total = 3.073e-38 cm²/nucleon (paper: 3.039e-38; ours runs 1.12 % high).
    68	- Strict-interior χ²/ndf vs paper (185 bins, full cov) = 3.549.
    69	- All-reported-bins χ²/ndf (205 bins) = 3.565.
    70	- Median bin ratio (ours/paper, strict interior) = 1.0084.
    71	- Bins within 5/10/20 % of paper (185 strict interior) = 81.1 / 95.7 / 98.4 %.
    72	- Global OmniFold input completeness c = 1.000000 (exact by construction).
    73	
    74	**Defining properties of the Phase-18.2 pipeline:**
    75	1. **Truth-tree-authoritative reco gate.** Event loop walks `mc_truth_denom`
    76	   first to build a `(mc_run, mc_subrun, mc_nthEvtInFile)` key set, then
    77	   walks reco and fills `mc_signal_reco` only when the key is in the set.
    78	   Truth-only events are appended as native OmniFold miss entries.
    79	2. **Bilateral upstream-dup dedupe.** Both loops dedupe on the same key
    80	   (handles 1,102 truth + 7 reco duplicates from one upstream-double-filled
    81	   AnaTuple, `MasterAnaDev_mc_AnaTuple_run00111353_Playlist.root`).
    82	3. **By-construction completeness.** `mc_signal_reco` entries ==
    83	   `mc_truth_denom` entries (32,849,103 each at MEFHC). The Phase-16 c
    84	   division in `unfold_2d_omnifold_unbinned.py` becomes a no-op self-check.
    85	
    86	**Residual disagreement.** The remaining ~3 χ²/ndf is dominated by
    87	sub-2 % shape disagreement in the highest p_|| tails (paper / weighted
    88	reaches 1.15 at 40-60 GeV/c). Attributed to a small reweighter-detail
    89	effect at high E_ν. Method-blindness confirmed by IBU 1D-projection
    90	cross-check (post-Phase-16): IBU and OmniFold-2D agree on the same inputs
    91	to ~1.7 %, both reproduce paper to within ~1-2 %.

    95	### Environment
    96	From the repo root (`MINERvA-OmniFold/`):
    97	```bash

   106	conda-24.10.0 base that created it — *not* `module load python; conda activate root_6_28`,
   107	which broke on 2026-07-02 when the default `python` module moved to a newer conda base that
   108	no longer registers the env by name (`EnvironmentNameNotFound`). Override with
   109	`ROOT628_PREFIX` / `ROOT628_CONDA` if the env or base moves. sbatch/salloc set `HOME` to the
   110	real home so `$HOME` resolves; for Claude's redirected-HOME **interactive** shell, source the
   111	scratch `rootenv.sh` (adds a `HOME=` override) instead. Legacy `module load python` path
   112	remains as a fallback if the prefix is absent.

   147	## 2D Python contract (must hold)
   148	1. **Mask measured data to phase space** (0 ≤ p_T ≤ 4.5,
   149	   1.5 ≤ p_|| ≤ 60) before step-1 training.
   150	2. **Subtract background** to build a non-negative reco target;
   151	   pass per-event `measured_weights` into `ohf.omnifold(...)`.
   152	3. **Include signal fakes in the reco-side subtraction.** Fakes (reco in
   153	   phase space, truth out) appear in `mc_signal_reco` but are filtered by
   154	   `omnifold.py`'s `MC_pass_truth_mask`. Add their POT-scaled reco weights
   155	   into `hBkgReco2D` so both measured and MC reco sides are fake-free.
   156	   Under Phase 18.2 the truth-tree-authoritative reco gate makes this set
   157	   empty in practice — `passesReco && !inPhaseSpace` is vacuous on MINERvA
   158	   AnaTuples — but the code path is retained as a regression check.
   159	4. **`hUnfold2D` is a truth-space event yield.** Fill with
   160	   `step2_weights * truth_w_in`, not raw `step2_weights`.
   161	5. **Divide by `hOFCompleteness2D`** (`hOFInputTruth2D / hOFTruthDenom2D`),
   162	   not by absolute selection efficiency `hEff2D`. Under Phase 18.2 this
   163	   ratio is ≡ 1 by construction; the division is a no-op self-check.
   164	6. **Closure mode** (`--closure`) restricts pseudo-data to
   165	   `pass_reco & pass_truth` and uses `sig["w_reco"]` as `measured_weights`
   166	   when `--use-weights`. Sets completeness ≡ 1.
   167	7. **Production sbatch must pass `--use-weights`** to match the 1A
   168	   validation footing.

   178	## NERSC SLURM gotchas
   179	
   180	### Running past the 3-hour interactive limit — use `alloc_run.sh`
   181	The agent runs on the **login node** (start it inside `tmux`/`screen` so it
   182	survives disconnects), NOT inside an salloc shell. An salloc shell dies at the
   183	180-min `interactive` limit and would take the agent with it. Instead, dispatch
   184	every compute command through the wrapper:
   185	```bash
   186	./alloc_run.sh '<command>'        # quote the whole command if it has pipes/&&/redirects
   187	./alloc_run.sh --status           # show the shared allocation
   188	./alloc_run.sh --end              # release it (scancel)
   189	```
   190	- It holds **exactly one** shared allocation (job-name `claude-hold`, guarded by
   191	  a job-name check + `flock`), detached via `setsid` so it outlives the shell.
   192	- It **reuses** that allocation across calls and **auto-requests a fresh one**
   193	  when the previous 3-hour allocation has expired — so total wall-clock is
   194	  unbounded while each underlying allocation stays ≤ 3 h.
   195	- The command runs on the compute node via `srun --jobid=<JOB> --overlap`, from
   196	  the repo root, with `setup_salloc_env.sh` already sourced (env chatter goes to
   197	  stderr; the command's stdout stays clean).
   198	- **Never** launch a bare interactive `salloc`/`source start_alloc.sh` for agent
   199	  work, and never start a second holder — one allocation at a time. Tune with
   200	  `ALLOC_HOLD_SECONDS` / `ALLOC_CPUS` / `ALLOC_JOB_NAME` if needed.
   201	- **Leave the allocation running between commands.** Do NOT call `--end` just
   202	  because a command finished — the next command reuses the live node, and
   203	  tearing it down only to re-request one wastes queue time and compute on
   204	  repeated start/stop churn. Only run `./alloc_run.sh --end` when the user
   205	  explicitly says they're done / closing the session, or asks to free the node.
   206	  Absent any such signal, keep it up; an idle allocation self-expires at the
   207	  3-hour limit anyway, and the next dispatch transparently requests a fresh one.
   208	- This is for interactive-style work that fits one node. Multi-node arrays /
   209	  multi-hour walls still go through `sbatch` (see below).
   210	
   211	- **Use the running interactive allocation when one exists, instead of
   212	  submitting a new sbatch.** The regular and shared queues at NERSC
   213	  routinely sit on `Priority` for several minutes to hours. If
   214	  `squeue -u $USER` shows a live `interactive` job with time remaining,
   215	  any task that fits inside that node (the build job, a short event-loop
   216	  smoke, a single short-wall unfold, a Python analysis script) should
   217	  run there via `srun --jobid=<INTERACTIVE_JOBID> --overlap -n 1
   218	  --cpus-per-task=<N> bash -lc '...'`. **Cancel the equivalent queued
   219	  sbatch** if you submitted one before noticing the interactive — running
   220	  in two places leaks compute and risks output-file races. Reserve fresh
   221	  sbatch submissions for jobs that don't fit a single interactive
   222	  allocation (multi-node arrays, multi-hour walls, or work that needs to
   223	  outlive your shell). The submit-vs-interactive decision is also
   224	  documented in `2d-unfolding/2D_OMNIFOLD_REFERENCE.md`.
   225	- Do NOT combine `set -u` with `conda activate root_6_28`. The conda
   226	  `deactivate-root.sh` hook references `CONDA_BACKUP_ROOTSYS` and aborts
   227	  under nounset in a fresh batch shell.
   228	- Export `PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1` so Python stdout flushes to `.out` live.
   229	- Do NOT use `srun` inside 2D OmniFold sbatch scripts. Inherited
   230	  `SRUN_CPUS_PER_TASK` from a live interactive allocation breaks nested
   231	  srun. Bare `python ...` is the safe default. (The interactive-shell
   232	  case is the opposite: `srun --jobid=<INTERACTIVE> --overlap` is the
   233	  correct primitive for launching work onto the existing allocation.)
   234	- Templates: `sbatch_unfold_2d_MEFHC.sh` (full-node 128 CPU regular QOS),
   235	  `sbatch_iter_scan_2d.sh` (shared QOS 2 CPU).
   236

   311	### TTree schema
   312	All TTrees carry both p_T and p_|| branches:
   313	- `mc_truth_denom`: `MC` (p_T), `MC_pz` (p_||), `w_truth`
   314	- `mc_signal_reco`: `sim`, `sim_pz`, `sim_pass`, `w_reco`, `MC`, `MC_pz`, `w_truth`
   315	- `mc_background`: `sim_background`, `sim_background_pz`, `sim_background_pass`, `w_bkg`
   316	- `data`: `measured`, `measured_pz`, `measured_pass`
   317	
   318	Metadata (`TParameter<double>`): `mcPOTUsed`, `dataPOTUsed`.
   319	Phase-17/18 additions (`TParameter<int>`/`<long>`): `hasTruthOnlyMisses`,
   320	`nTruthOnlyMisses`. No `pTmu_fiducial_nucleons` (intentional — see hadd
   321	note above).

   323	### Paper bin edges (authoritative; matches `bin_mapping.txt`)
   324	```python

   432	## Running compute on interactive nodes (salloc) — hard-won lessons (2026-06-04)
   433	
   434	When the batch (shared/regular) queue is fairshare-throttled (you get only ~1-2 slots after
   435	running many jobs), `qos=interactive` is a SEPARATE queue that grabs a fresh node fast. It is
   436	worth using for **many small concurrent jobs that fit on one node** (e.g. lean npz-based
   437	bank-unfolds, or the ~12 per-playlist event loops). It is NOT a substitute for a large array
   438	(can't fit hundreds at once; interactive is time-/alloc-limited, typically <=2 allocs, <=4h).
   439	
   440	Pitfalls that cost real time here — DO NOT repeat:
   441	
   442	1. **Run the orchestrator INSIDE the salloc.** `salloc --qos interactive ... bash orchestrator.sh`
   443	   where the script launches up to ~10 concurrent `srun --overlap --exact -n1 -cN ...` steps,
   444	   then `wait`. Working examples: `nd-unfolding/run_q3_sweep_interactive.sh`,
   445	   `run_pc_evloop_interactive.sh`. Do NOT salloc-then-external-`srun --jobid=<id> --overlap`
   446	   from another shell: it races node configuration and fails with
   447	   "Unable to create step ... Invalid job id specified".
   448	
   449	2. **MONITOR BY OUTPUT ARTIFACTS, NOT salloc stdout.** salloc/srun stdout is heavily BUFFERED
   450	   when backgrounded/non-tty, so a perfectly healthy run shows an EMPTY log for many minutes.
   451	   A working q3 sweep was once CANCELLED because the log looked "stuck" — it was actually
   452	   mid-processing (visible only via `pgrep -af srun` and the produced `.root` files). Track
   453	   progress by counting output files + `squeue -j <allocid>` liveness; give each job its full
   454	   runtime before judging. Use skip-if-exists so cancel/relaunch is always safe & resumable.
   455	
   456	3. **Clean up holders.** `alloc_run.sh --end` scancels the SLURM job but the login-node
   457	   `salloc ... sleep` CLIENT process can linger and accumulate. After --end, verify with
   458	   `squeue --me --name=claude-hold` AND `pgrep -u $USER -af 'qos interactive'`; scancel/kill
   459	   leftovers so the queue stays clean.
   460	
   461	4. **Never background salloc with a bare `&`** from a returning shell (SIGHUP risk). Use the
   462	   harness `run_in_background`, or `nohup`/`setsid`.
   463	
   464	5. The `alloc_run.sh` wrapper (one held node, reused across calls, auto-renews past 3h) is the
   465	   right tool for SERIAL on-demand validation/plotting (login node can't run ROOT/LGBM).
   466	   For PARALLEL throughput, use the inside-salloc orchestrator pattern above instead.

     1	# PET UQ remediation — dependency map
     2	
     3	**Scope:** PET point-cloud central value and uncertainty budget only. This file
     4	defines ordering and completion gates; job ownership and live execution remain
     5	in session-specific state files. Verified numbers belong in
     6	`../VALIDATION_LEDGER.md` only after their commit lands.
     7	
     8	## Bottom line
     9	
    10	Do **not** begin with full per-universe PET retraining. Every such training
    11	depends on the corrected background-subtracted measured target and nominal PET
    12	configuration. First build and validate that target, retrain the nominal model,
    13	and measure the ordinary statistical/ML spreads. Then use a small targeted set
    14	of systematic retrains to determine whether the frozen-reweighter approximation
    15	is adequate. Expand to more universes only if that response is material.
    16	
    17	The existing unsubtracted PET input, nominal weights, eight bootstrap replicas,
    18	and dependent covariances are cross-checks only. None may enter the corrected
    19	budget.
    20	
    21	## Dimensionality decision (gate 0)
    22	
    23	One point-cloud input cannot exactly reproduce both existing scalar purity
    24	targets: the 4D and 5D `measured_weights` differ on 3,836,331 of 4,091,707 data
    25	events (maximum absolute difference 0.838).
    26	
    27	Recommended production definition:
    28	
    29	- use `of_inputs_5d.npz` measured weights as the canonical 5D PET target;
    30	- build one coherent corrected 5D PET estimator and budget;
    31	- obtain 4D as an exact covariance marginal/projection of that 5D result and
    32	  label it **the 4D marginal of the 5D PET estimator**.
    33	
    34	Alternative (roughly doubles GPU work): train separate 4D- and 5D-target PET
    35	ensembles. This is required only if PET must match each separately trained
    36	scalar target rather than present one coherent 5D measurement and its marginal.
    37	Record the choice before launching the ensemble.
    38	
    39	## Ordered DAG
    40	
    41	### 1. Corrected measured target (CPU/input work)
    42	
    43	1. Preserve `of_inputs_pc_fullcloud.npz` unchanged as the unsubtracted control.
    44	2. Create a separately named background-subtracted input. Reuse the existing
    45	   complete cloud tensors/source omnifile; no new C++ event loop is needed.
    46	3. Store or reconstruct the data scalar coordinates and prove event-by-event
    47	   row alignment against the chosen scalar NPZ before copying its purity
    48	   weights. Equal row counts alone are not an alignment proof.
    49	4. Require finite weights in `[0,1]`; reproduce the scalar target sum and
    50	   per-bin `max(0, data-background)/data` factors exactly.
    51	5. Add a small complete end-to-end fixture that reaches PET extraction under
    52	   the ROOT environment.
    53	
    54	**Gate:** row alignment exact; target histogram and total weight exact; old
    55	input untouched; input provenance stored in a JSON/txt summary.
    56	
    57	### 2. Corrected nominal PET (GPU)
    58	
    59	Train one unbootstrapped nominal PET model on the corrected input with the final
    60	training-event count, iterations, epochs, estimator seed, and subsample seed.
    61	Extract its 5D cross section and chosen projections. All later blocks must embed
    62	this same reported-bin mask/order and corrected nominal central vector.
    63	
    64	**Gate:** finite full-coverage weights, strict ordered MC indices, extraction
    65	passes, dimensional marginal/normalization checks pass.
    66	
    67	### 3. Corrected GPU floor (GPU)
    68	
    69	Repeat the exact nominal configuration once. This measures nondeterminism for
    70	the corrected target; the old unsubtracted duplicate is not transferable.
    71	
    72	**Gate:** floor recorded before interpreting either `C_stat` or `C_ML`.
    73	
    74	### 4. Independent statistical and ML ensembles (GPU; may run in parallel)
    75	
    76	- **`C_stat`:** vary only the independent data/MC Poisson replica ID. Hold the
    77	  estimator and training-subsample seeds fixed. Apply the identical coherent MC
    78	  draw in training and final counts/completeness. Center covariance on the
    79	  replica mean and require an exact manifest.
    80	- **PET `C_ML`:** no Poisson fluctuation. Use the corrected input and vary PET
    81	  training randomness. Prefer a small crossed design over subsample/split seed
    82	  and TensorFlow estimator seed so their interaction is visible. The GBDT
    83	  `C_ML` scan does not substitute for this PET ensemble.
    84	
    85	Run pilots first; scale only after both spreads exceed the corrected GPU floor
    86	by an interpretable margin and the replica extraction gate passes.
    87	
    88	### 5. Rebuild frozen-reweighter systematic blocks (CPU/large-memory)
    89	
    90	Using the corrected nominal PET push weights:
    91	
    92	1. rebuild the vertical `C_syst` with actual minus/plus endpoints, exact 100-ID
    93	   flux inventory, MAT mean-centered biased `1/N`, and the agreed invalid-ratio
    94	   policy;
    95	2. rebuild the PET-native lateral block on selection-complete universe inputs;
    96	3. rerun the corrected PET unified/block diagnostic with individual-factor
    97	   clipping but no composite re-clip.
    98	
    99	The clean truth-ratio bank may support a **preliminary vertical** block because
   100	the frozen PET calculation consumes truth ratios only. A publication-final
   101	claim must still respect `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` #13/#16: use the new background-aware
   102	dump/re-bank and selection-complete lateral products owned by the shared GBDT
   103	campaign when available; do not independently duplicate that event-loop work.
   104	
   105	### 6. Targeted per-universe retraining-response study (GPU decision gate)
   106	
   107	Only now retrain a small, predeclared set of dominant/kinematic systematic
   108	endpoints. Include representatives of:
   109	
   110	- the dominant vertical model band(s);
   111	- flux (leading/effective modes rather than all 100 replicas for the pilot);
   112	- the dominant muon-energy/beam lateral endpoint(s), using genuinely shifted
   113	  clouds, selection, and background target.
   114	
   115	For each endpoint compare the fully retrained displacement with the matched
   116	frozen-reweighter displacement, after subtracting/bounding the GPU floor. Set
   117	the materiality criterion before looking at results (trace change plus
   118	per-bin-tail criterion).
   119	
   120	Decision:
   121	
   122	- **Compatible within the declared tolerance:** retain the frozen-reweighter
   123	  block, quote the targeted response study as its validation/bound, and disclose
   124	  that the full universe set was not retrained.
   125	- **Material for particular bands:** retrain those bands or construct an
   126	  explicitly documented response correction/envelope; repeat the gate.
   127	- **Broadly material:** only then consider a full per-universe campaign. Full
   128	  187-universe PET retraining is not the default and requires an explicit GPU
   129	  cost/benefit decision.
   130	
   131	### 7. Final assembly and commit gate
   132	
   133	Assemble, on one matched corrected PET central and ordering,
   134	
   135	`C_total = C_syst + C_stat + C_ML + C_lateral`.
   136	
   137	Before calling it final, require:
   138	
   139	- exact manifests and common central/mask/order checks for every block;
   140	- covariance symmetry, PSD/eigen diagnostics, finite diagonal, and exact 5D to
   141	  4D projection checks;
   142	- corrected GPU-floor comparison and closure/extraction validation;
   143	- unified/block and targeted-retraining verdicts stated without silently
   144	  adopting an old inflation;
   145	- products summary plus ledger, RUN_LOG, and STATUS updates in the same commit.
   146	
   147	Until gate 7 lands, use “corrected PET component/pilot” rather than “final PET
   148	budget,” and retain the old eight replicas only as an unsubtracted cross-check.
   149	
   150	## Reuse / rerun matrix
   151	
   152	| Item | Disposition |
   153	|---|---|
   154	| Complete point-cloud source omnifile/cloud tensors | Reuse after row-alignment proof |
   155	| Flux, POT, nucleon constant, GBDT completeness anchors | Reuse; keep provenance fixed |
   156	| Scalar 5D background-subtracted measured weights | Reuse as canonical target (recommended) |
   157	| Existing unsubtracted PC NPZ and eight replicas | Preserve, label cross-check only |
   158	| Old PET nominal/alternate weights | Do not use in corrected blocks |
   159	| Corrected nominal and floor duplicate | Rerun |
   160	| PET `C_stat` and PET-specific `C_ML` | Rerun independently |
   161	| PET vertical, lateral, unified/block products | Rebuild with corrected nominal weights |
   162	| Selection-complete universes/new background-aware bank | Reuse shared GBDT campaign products when they land |
   163	| Full per-universe PET retraining | Defer pending targeted response gate |

1	# 2D OmniFold Study — Reference Notes
     2	
     3	Workflow invariants, recurring gotchas, and correctness contracts. Read
     4	before launching jobs or trusting outputs.
     5	
     6	---
     7	
     8	## Environment setup
     9	
    10	From the repo root (`MINERvA-OmniFold/`):
    11	
    12	```bash
    13	source setup_salloc_env.sh
    14	```
    15	
    16	That script is self-locating: it loads the `root_6_28` conda env, sources
    17	`unbinned_unfolding/build/setup.sh` (built in-tree), exports
    18	`MINERVA_PREFIX=$REPO/MINERvA101/opt`, and sources
    19	`MINERvA101/opt/bin/setup.sh`. No HOME paths involved.
    20	
    21	## Archiving conventions
    22	
    23	Stale logs and one-off artifacts that we keep for reference but don't want
    24	in the active tree go to a dated archive directory at the OLD scratch root,
    25	**outside the new repo**:
    26	
    27	```
    28	/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA101/archive_pre_migration_<YYYY-MM-DD>/
    29	```
    30	
    31	Rules:
    32	
    33	- Active outputs (current `.root`, `.png`, recent job logs) live in
    34	  `2d-unfolding/`.
    35	- Once superseded, move job logs (`*_<jobid>.{out,err}`), throwaway dirs
    36	  (`Doc_tmp/`, scratch scripts), and orphaned PDFs into a new dated
    37	  `archive_pre_migration_*` (or `archive_<purpose>_*`) folder under the OLD
    38	  scratch root.
    39	- In-tree archives are gitignored via `2d-unfolding/archive_*/` (see
    40	  `.gitignore`). Anything still in the OLD scratch tree is outside git
    41	  entirely.
    42	- Heavy intermediates (`runEventLoopOmniFold_*.root`, `evloop_work_*/`)
    43	  are gitignored by the global `*.root` rule and `evloop_work_*/` rule;
    44	  they may be copied into `2d-unfolding/` as needed without being tracked.
    45	
    46	Canonical runtime binary: `opt/bin/runEventLoopOmniFold` (the path exported
    47	by `opt/bin/setup.sh`). Do **not** call build-tree copies — duplicate
    48	build-tree executables have silently shadowed the installed patched binary
    49	in the past.
    50	
    51	## SLURM script conventions
    52	
    53	- **Interactive-first.** Before submitting an sbatch, check
    54	  `squeue -u $USER` for a live `interactive` allocation with time left.
    55	  NERSC's regular and shared queues routinely sit on `Priority` for
    56	  minutes-to-hours; an existing interactive allocation can run the same
    57	  work immediately. Use
    58	  `srun --jobid=<INTERACTIVE_JOBID> --overlap -n 1 --cpus-per-task=<N>
    59	  bash -lc '... ; source $REPO/setup_salloc_env.sh ; <command>'`
    60	  to launch onto it. Work that fits this pattern includes: the C++
    61	  build (`cmake --build && cmake --install`, ~5 min, 8-16 CPU), short
    62	  1A event-loop smokes (~5-15 min, 8-128 CPU), individual unfold runs,
    63	  Python analysis scripts. The interactive's full 128 CPU is available
    64	  inside one node even if your `salloc` requested fewer — `srun
    65	  --cpus-per-task=N` controls the slice. If you already submitted an
    66	  sbatch and *then* realized the interactive is up, `scancel` the
    67	  sbatch — leaving both running leaks compute and risks output-file
    68	  races. Reserve sbatch for genuinely multi-node arrays, multi-hour
    69	  walls, or work that must outlive the shell.
    70	- Do **not** combine `set -u` with `conda activate root_6_28`. The conda
    71	  `deactivate-root.sh` hook references `CONDA_BACKUP_ROOTSYS` and aborts
    72	  under nounset in a fresh batch shell.
    73	- Export `PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1` so Python stdout appears in the `.out` file
    74	  during the run.
    75	- **Do not use `srun`** inside 2D OmniFold sbatch scripts. On Perlmutter,
    76	  inherited `SRUN_CPUS_PER_TASK` from a live interactive allocation breaks
    77	  nested srun. Bare `python3 ...` is the safe default. (The interactive
    78	  case is the inverse: `srun --jobid=<INTERACTIVE> --overlap` is the
    79	  correct primitive when launching new work onto an existing allocation.)
    80	- See `sbatch_validate_1A_corrected.sh` and `sbatch_unfold_2d.sh` for the
    81	  working template.
    82	
    83	## Event-loop workflow
    84	
    85	- `runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp` picks **one** global playlist flux/calibration
    86	  from the first event's run number. **Never** feed it a combined MEFHC
    87	  manifest — the tool silently applies the first playlist's flux to all
    88	  events, corrupting 11/12 of the dataset.
    89	  → Run per-playlist, then `hadd` outputs.
    90	- `runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp` intentionally does **not** write
    91	  `pTmu_fiducial_nucleons` because `hadd` sums `TParameter<double>`
    92	  objects, inflating the detector-geometry nucleon constant by 12×. The
    93	  Python extraction uses the fixed tracker-geometry constant 3.2353e30.
    94	- Old pre-fix merged OmniFold ROOT files may carry summed
    95	  `pTmu_fiducial_nucleons` metadata — treat as untrustworthy.
    96	- **`hadd` 100 GB TTree trap (large dump-all / `_universes_full` merges):**
    97	  plain `hadd -f` ABORTS when the merged `mc_signal_reco`/`mc_truth_denom`
    98	  trees exceed ROOT's default 100 GB `TTree::fgMaxTreeSize`. ROOT rolls over
    99	  to a continuation file (`..._1.root`) mid-merge and the `TFileMerger`
   100	  fatals: *"Output file ... has been deleted (likely due to a TTree larger
   101	  than 100Gb)"* → SIGABRT (exit 134), leaving a ~94 GB partial **missing the
   102	  `data` + `mc_background` trees** (they merge last). Hit on the 2D dump-all
   103	  (~119 GB) and the 3D dump-all (~120 GB), same 94 GB signature both times.
   104	  `hadd` has no flag to raise the limit, so use the Python `TFileMerger` that
   105	  bumps `TTree::SetMaxTreeSize` to 300 GB instead:
   106	  `uq/hadd_universes_full.py OUT INPUT...` (generic; serves 2D and 3D), wired
   107	  in `sbatch_hadd_*_universes_full.sh` with `mem=64G`. The merge is fast-method
   108	  (basket copy, I/O-bound, ~7 min for 120 GB). **Never** bare-`hadd` a
   109	  `_universes_full` omnifile.
   110	- `runEventLoop.cpp` (baseline histogram path) is where
   111	  `pTmu_reweightedflux_integrated` is written;
   112	  `runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp` does **not** write the flux histogram. Use
   113	  `sbatch_runEventLoop_baseline_flux_array.sh` to regenerate per-playlist
   114	  baseline flux files, then `combine_flux_MEFHC.py` to build the
   115	  POT-weighted MEFHC flux (`baseline_flux/runEventLoopMC_MEFHC.root`).
   116	
   117	## 2D Python unfolding contract
   118	
   119	`2d-unfolding/unfold_2d_omnifold_unbinned.py` is the authoritative 2D unbinned
   120	OmniFold extraction path. Required invariants:
   121	
   122	1. **Mask measured data to phase space** (0 ≤ p_T ≤ 4.5, 1.5 ≤ p_|| ≤ 60)
   123	   before step-1 training.
   124	2. **Subtract background** — build non-negative reco-space target from
   125	   `data − bkg` and pass per-event `measured_weights` into
   126	   `ohf.omnifold(...)`.
   127	3. **Include signal fakes in the reco-side subtraction.** Fakes
   128	   (reco in phase space, truth out) appear in `mc_signal_reco` but are
   129	   filtered by `omnifold.py`'s `MC_pass_truth_mask`. Add their POT-scaled
   130	   reco weights into `hBkgReco2D` so both measured and MC reco sides are
   131	   fake-free.
   132	4. **`hUnfold2D` is a truth-space event yield.** Fill it with
   133	   `step2_weights * truth_w_in`, not raw `step2_weights`.
   134	5. **Do not divide `hUnfold2D` by `hEff2D`** when building `hXSec2D`.
   135	   OmniFold returns an efficiency-corrected truth spectrum on the
   136	   truth-selected sample. `hEff2D` is diagnostic only.
   137	6. **Closure mode** must restrict pseudo-data to `pass_reco & pass_truth`
   138	   and use `sig["w_reco"]` as `measured_weights` when `--use-weights` is
   139	   on, matching the `MCreco_weights` footing.
   140	7. **Full-stats sbatch must pass `--use-weights`.** The 1A validation runs
   141	   use it; the production path must match.
   142	
   143	## Bootstrap-replica workflow (`--bootstrap-seed N`)
   144	
   145	Per-event Poisson(1) weight bootstrap on data + MC jointly. Invariants:
   146	
   147	1. **Two independent sub-RNGs per replica** —
   148	   `np.random.default_rng(seed)` for data, `np.random.default_rng(seed +
   149	   10_000_000)` for MC. Data-stat and MC-stat are independent variance
   150	   sources; they must not share a draw or the covariance under-counts.
   151	2. **MC reco and MC truth weights ride a single per-event MC draw.**
   152	   `sig["w_truth"]` and `sig["w_reco"]` are multiplied by the *same*
   153	   per-event Poisson factor — they're two views of the same MC event and
   154	   must stay correlated within the event row. Using independent draws
   155	   for w_truth and w_reco would smear out the migration matrix.
   156	3. **`--bootstrap-seed` + `--closure` is coverage-toy mode only.**
   157	   Closure mode copies `sig["w_reco"]` into `measured_weights` before
   158	   the bootstrap multiply; adding independent data/MC Poisson draws
   159	   intentionally breaks the strict closure identity. Use this only for
   160	   pseudo-experiment coverage calibration, not for deterministic closure
   161	   tests.
   162	4. **Pin the GBT random_state too.** Each replica should pass both
   163	   `--bootstrap-seed N` and `--seed N` so ML stochasticity is removed
   164	   from the per-replica variance. The bootstrap variance and the
   165	   seedscan variance are *separable* uncorrelated components only when
   166	   each replica's GBT seed is pinned.
   167	5. **CV unfold = omit the flag.** Don't pass `--bootstrap-seed 0` and
   168	   call it the CV; seed=0 is a valid replica with a non-trivial Poisson
   169	   draw. The CV is the unflagged run.
   170	
   171	Driver: `uq/run_bootstrap_interactive.sh` runs N replicas inside an
   172	existing interactive allocation, batched WIDTH-wide ×
   173	(128/WIDTH)-threads. **Use WIDTH=1 on Perlmutter single-node interactive
   174	allocations.** The 2026-05-19 contention lesson (sklearn HistGBT
   175	bandwidth-bound at WIDTH≥2) has not been re-benchmarked for lgbm; even
   176	though lgbm scales fine to 128 threads on a single MEFHC unfold,
   177	WIDTH>1 packed onto one node is unmeasured. Until benchmarked, run
   178	sequential at full node width.
   179	
   180	Analyzer: `uq/analyze_uq.py` rolls N replica ROOTs into per-bin
   181	mean/std, 205×205 covariance on paper-reported bins, and 1D pT/pz
   182	projection covariances. The Cholesky PD check uses a small jitter
   183	fallback because the covariance is rank-deficient by construction
   184	whenever N ≤ n_reported (205); a meaningful PD test needs N >
   185	n_reported or pooling across replica sets. Quote the 205 paper-reported
   186	bins everywhere, not the full 14×16 grid.
   187	
   188	## Universe-covariance workflow (`--universe BAND:IDX`)
   189	
   190	Universe covariance is computed from per-universe deltas relative to a
   191	matched CV unfold. The CV must use the same omnifile, estimator,
   192	`--seed`, iteration count, `--use-weights` setting, and flux input as the
   193	universe sweep; only `--universe` is omitted. Mixing an exact-GBT
   194	production CV with lgbm universe outputs injects backend/seed baseline
   195	differences into every paired systematic covariance.
   196	
   197	For the full lateral+vertical MEFHC sweep:
   198	
   199	- Universe sweep:
   200	  `sbatch_unfold_2d_MEFHC_5iter_universes_full.sh` →

201	  `uq/2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_<BAND>_<IDX>.root`.
   202	- Matched CV:
   203	  `sbatch_unfold_2d_MEFHC_5iter_universes_full_CV.sh` →
   204	  `uq/2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_CV.root`.
   205	- Rollup:
   206	  `sbatch_final_rollup_full.sh`, whose driver
   207	  `uq/final_rollup_full.sh` refuses to run without the matched full-CV
   208	  ROOT and archives any superseded baseline-mismatched full-rollup
   209	  artifacts before writing replacements.
   210	- Plots:
   211	  `uq_universe_band_pt.png` and `uq_universe_band_pz.png` show grouped
   212	  categories, not one line per universe band. Categories are Flux,
   213	  Models, Normalization, Statistical, Hadronic response, and Muon
   214	  reconstruction. The Statistical line appears when a bootstrap
   215	  covariance is supplied to `analyze_universes.py`.
   216	- Paper-Fig.-6/7-style fractional uncertainty projections are made with
   217	  `uq/plot_uncertainty_fig6_7_style.py`. Unlike the quick grouped sigma
   218	  diagnostic in `analyze_universes.py`, this script projects the full
   219	  205x205 covariance exactly, `C_1D = P C_2D P^T`, before dividing by
   220	  the reported-bin 1D central value. It writes
   221	  `uq/universe_stage2_MEFHC_full/MEFHC_fig6_7_uncertainty_{pz,pt}.png`
   222	  and a numeric summary text file. ML covariance is included in the
   223	  total when available, but is drawn only when it exceeds the configured
   224	  visibility threshold.
   225	
   226	All systematic bands use the MAT covariance convention implemented by
   227	`MnvVertErrorBand::CalcCovMx`: center universes on their universe mean and
   228	normalize by biased `1/N`. For a +/- pair this is the outer product of the
   229	half-difference; a common endpoint displacement from CV is reported separately
   230	rather than counted as variance. Multi-universe bands use the same formula. Inverse-cov
   231	diagnostics for an ours-only covariance are expected to be fragile when
   232	finite universe ensembles underspan the 205 reported-bin space; quote
   233	direct per-bin pulls and truncated-mode chi2 alongside any pseudo-inverse
   234	chi2.
   235	
   236	`--universe BAND:IDX` on a dump-all omnifile remains limited to CV event-loop
   237	support. When shifted selection can move events into or out of the sample, use
   238	per-playlist `MNV101_ACTIVE_UNIVERSE=BAND:IDX`, merge the ordinary trees, and
   239	require migration metadata plus finite-support signal/denominator closure. The
   240	five kinematic bands are BeamAngleX/Y, MuonResolution, and
   241	Muon_Energy_MINERvA/MINOS; MinosEfficiency and GEANT are weight-only.
   242	
   243	## Flux and normalization
   244	
   245	- The paper scalar `6.32e-8 /cm²/POT` is **not** the same quantity as the
   246	  flux normalization term used by this code path. Substituting it blows up
   247	  the total xsec by ~13.8×.
   248	- Outputs embed `hFlux_pt` and `fluxSource` so later auditing doesn't
   249	  depend on reopening an external ROOT file.
   250	- Playlist-dependent flux differences are a ~0.2 % effect; not a dominant
   251	  systematic.
   252	
   253	## Paper / ancillary comparison
   254	
   255	- No HepData entry. Authoritative target: `2d-unfolding/minerva_paper_anc/`.
   256	- Use `bin_mapping.txt`, not the axis labels on the ancillary TH2D.
   257	- `pzb=1` = first p_|| bin, 1.5 < p_|| < 2.0 GeV/c (the fragile low-p_||
   258	  MINOS range-out regime).
   259	
   260	## `IsMinosMatchMuon` (current state; our edit 2026-04-25)
   261	
   262	The upstream MINERvA-101 tutorial `main` now does a real MINOS match:
   263	
   264	```cpp
   265	virtual bool IsMinosMatchMuon() const {
   266	  return GetIsMinosMatchTrack() == 1;
   267	}
   268	```
   269	
   270	Our `CVUniverse.h:107` does the same `isMinosMatchTrack==1` match and adds the
   271	`minos_trk_is_ok==1` fit-quality bit:
   272	
   273	```cpp
   274	return GetInt("isMinosMatchTrack") == 1 &&
   275	       GetInt(GetAnaToolName() + "_minos_trk_is_ok") == 1;
   276	```
   277	
   278	(An early tutorial revision instead used `has_interaction_vertex==1`, an
   279	educational stub true for essentially every event in the pre-selected
   280	AnaTuple, which biased the low-p_|| cross section low. Our 2026-04-25 edit
   281	predates the upstream tutorial's own fix; both now enforce the real match.)
   282	
   283	This requires a MINOS-matched muon track with a passing MINOS fit.
   284	AnaTuple branches verified on `MasterAnaDev_mc_AnaTuple_run00110000_Playlist.root`:
   285	`isMinosMatchTrack` (Int_t) ∈ {−1, 1}; `MasterAnaDev_minos_trk_is_ok`
   286	(Bool_t) ∈ {0, 1}.
   287	
   288	Any pre-patch event-loop outputs (`runEventLoopOmniFold_*.root` from before
   289	2026-04-25) include the looser selection and must be regenerated before
   290	they can be compared to paper numbers.
   291	
   292	**Note**: this fix removed a real selection bug (background rate dropped
   293	from ~10 % to 0.35 %, matching the paper's ~0.2 %), but the residual
   294	low-p_|| sum-ratio gradient (0.6 at p_||=1.5–2 GeV/c rising to ~1.0
   295	above p_||=20 GeV/c) **persists** after the fix. The gradient is not
   296	caused by MINOS-match selection; it is most likely a MINOS geometric
   297	acceptance / range-out efficiency that the MINERvA-101 tutorial path
   298	does not implement.
   299	
   300	**Fixability assessment (2026-06-10, KNOWN_ISSUES #5)**: the
   301	`MINOSEfficiencyReweighter` (intensity-dependent data/MC matching
   302	efficiency, few-%) IS already applied in our MnvTune stack
   303	(`runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp:1113`) — far too small to explain the
   304	gradient. The candidate was the official MasterAnaDev muon-quality
   305	selection the tutorial path omits. Scoping found dead-time already in
   306	the preCuts (`NoDeadtime(1)`) and `minos_trk_fit_pass` implied by the
   307	patched IsMinosMatchMuon (100% of matched events), leaving
   308	`minos_trk_quality==1` (23.5% of matched MC is quality-2) and the
   309	curvature-significance cut.
   310	
   311	**Diagnostic RESULT (2026-06-10, job 54280253,
   312	`minos_quality_diagnostic.py` → `products/minos_quality_diagnostic.png`)
   313	— quality cuts ACQUITTED.** Conditional efficiency of the added cuts
   314	among base-selected events (1A AnaTuples, 1.66M MC / 158k data):
   315	eff_data/eff_MC = 1.03–1.05 at p_MINOS 1–2.5 GeV/c vs 1.06–1.14 at
   316	10–60 (quality and quality+curvature alike). Closing the 0.6→1.0
   317	gradient needed ~1.67 at low p falling to 1.0 — the observed double
   318	ratio is far too small and has the wrong shape. Data is uniformly MORE
   319	efficient than MC for these cuts, so applying them cannot produce a
   320	low-p_|| data deficit at all. (Side finding: `minos_trk_eqp_qp` is the
   321	already-fractional q/p error — the /qp variant removes >99% of
   322	high-momentum events and is the wrong reading.) The gradient therefore
   323	remains attributed to MINOS acceptance/efficiency modeling upstream of
   324	selection (or generator rate mismodeling); its net effect inside the
   325	published phase space is bounded by the 2D paper reproduction (integral
   326	ratio 1.011), and the FPS p_||<1.5 region carries tier-2 flagging.
   327	
   328	## Output hygiene
   329	
   330	- Preserve known-bad but diagnostically useful files under an explicit
   331	  `archive_*/debug/` name before a rerun overwrites a canonical path.
   332	- Never leave invalidated outputs at canonical production paths once a
   333	  corrected rerun is complete.
   334	
   335	## 3D OmniFold extension (Workstream C)
   336	
   337	The 3D available-energy extension (`d³σ/(dp_T dp_‖ dE_avail)`) lives in the
   338	sibling `../3d-unfolding/` and **reuses this contract**: its driver
   339	`unfold_3d_omnifold_unbinned.py` does `import unfold_2d_omnifold_unbinned` and
   340	calls the 2D helpers, so everything above — POT scaling, `TRACKER_FIDUCIAL_N_NUCLEONS`,
   341	the `in_truth_phase_space` θ_μ<20° gate, the per-p_T flux convention, the
   342	`hXSec2D` naming for `compare_to_paper_fullcov.py` — applies unchanged. 3D-only
   343	invariants:
   344	
   345	- **Eavail axis defs** (arXiv:2312.16631 Eq. 4): truth `GetEAvailableTrue()`,
   346	  reco `NewEavail()` (tracker+ECAL ×1.17), added standalone to `CVUniverse.h`
   347	  (the MAT calculator headers redefine `GetVertex()`). MeV→GeV in the branches.
   348	- **Eavail binning catch bin**: the top edge must be large (default 100 GeV) so
   349	  the Eavail-marginal captures the full recoil tail — `Σ_k xsec·ΔE_k = 2D` only
   350	  if every truth event lands in a bin. A truncated top edge breaks the anchor.
   351	- **CV-only**: the 3D driver does NOT carry the universe / alt-model / bootstrap
   352	  machinery; that is the deferred 3D-UQ campaign.
   353	- **`compare_to_paper_fullcov.py` from 3d-unfolding/**: always pass
   354	  `--out-prefix`, or the default clobbers the tracked 2D `MEFHC_5iter_pull_full.png`.
   355	
   356	Its own docs (mirroring this triad): `../3d-unfolding/3D_OMNIFOLD_STATUS.md`
   357	(dashboard), `../3d-unfolding/3D_OMNIFOLD_RUN_LOG.md` (chronology),
   358	`../3d-unfolding/README.md` (orientation + how-to-run).
   359	
   360	## Documentation split
   361	
   362	Per-workstream mirror (see AGENTS.md). **2D** (this directory):
   363	- `2D_OMNIFOLD_STUDY_STATUS.md` — dashboard, current state, next actions.
   364	- `2D_OMNIFOLD_RUN_LOG.md` — append-only chronology.
   365	- `2D_OMNIFOLD_REFERENCE.md` (this file) — stable invariants and gotchas
   366	  (shared with 3D; see § "3D OmniFold extension" above).
   367	- `PLOT_GUIDE.md` — PNG reading guide.
   368	
   369	**3D** (`../3d-unfolding/`): `3D_OMNIFOLD_STATUS.md`, `3D_OMNIFOLD_RUN_LOG.md`,
   370	`README.md`. No separate 3D REFERENCE — invariants are shared here.

item.text: Two repository-wide constraints are directly relevant: resume-by-nonempty-file is a known unsafe pattern ([KNOWN_ISSUES.md:38–47]), and dump-all lateral universes remain CV-support-limited unless promoted selection-complete endpoints carry migration metadata and finite-support closure ([2D_OMNIFOLD_REFERENCE.md:226–241]). I’m now checking whether the standard P4 chain enforces those conditions in code and whether the local endpoint evidence actually satisfies them.

1	#!/bin/bash
     2	# STANDARD P4 stage-2: unfold the 10 merged endpoints -> flat 5D xsec vectors.
     3	# Bare-background (NO nested srun) so it runs under a single outer
     4	# `srun --overlap --jobid=<holder>` step. Nominal unfold per merged endpoint
     5	# (NO --universe; the ROOT already IS the promoted universe), FIXED --seed 42 on
     6	# both endpoints so the MAT +/- pair cancels the CV. valid-skip so it resumes
     7	# after a wall on a fresh holder. Writes active_universe_5d/standard/unfolds/.
     8	set -o pipefail
     9	export HOME=/global/homes/j/josephrb
    10	REPO="/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold"; ND="${REPO}/nd-unfolding"
    11	BANDS=(BeamAngleX BeamAngleY MuonResolution Muon_Energy_MINERvA Muon_Energy_MINOS)
    12	MERGEDIR="${ND}/active_universe_5d/standard/merged"
    13	OUTDIR="${ND}/active_universe_5d/standard/unfolds"; mkdir -p "${OUTDIR}"
    14	CONC="${CONC:-4}"
    15	echo "[p4-unfold] start $(date -u +%T) CONC=${CONC}"
    16	
    17	valid_unfold () {  # a complete unfold ROOT has hXSecND_flat

    20	unfold_one () {

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Universe-covariance rollup for the 5D (pt,pz,eavail,q3,W) OmniFold UQ campaign.
     3	
     4	5D-named sibling of analyze_universes_nd.py (identical math; only the output histogram
     5	names carry a 5d tag, e.g. hCov_combined5d_total). Reads the flat hXSecND_flat (C-order
     6	ravel over the 5D shape) from the CV unfold and the per-(band,idx) universe unfolds
     7	(sweep_bank_5d.py --run / sbatch_unfold_5d_detector.sh), builds the block-summed
     8	systematic covariance, an optional flat normalization band, and an optional block-sum
     9	with extra (stat/ML) covariances. Reported bins = CV>0.
    10	
    11	  python analyze_universes_5d.py --cv products/5d/xsec_5d_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm.root \
    12	      --glob 'uq_5d/universe_sweep/5d_xsec_*_uni_full_*.root' \
    13	      --add-norm 0.014 \
    14	      --bootstrap-cov uq_cov_stat_5d.root:hCov_stat5d_reported \
    15	                      uq_cov_mlsplit_5d.root:hCov_mlsplit5d_reported \
    16	      --outdir uq_5d/universe_stage2_5d/ --out-root uq_universe_5d_covariance_combined.root
    17	"""
    18	import argparse
    19	import glob
    20	import os
    21	import re
    22	from collections import defaultdict
    23	
    24	import numpy as np
    25	import ROOT
    26	
    27	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    28	UNI_RE = re.compile(r".*_uni(?:_full)?_(?P<band>[A-Za-z0-9_]+?)_(?P<idx>\d+)\.root$")
    29	
    30	CATEGORY_ORDER = ["Flux", "Models", "Normalization", "Hadronic response",
    31	                  "Muon reconstruction"]
    32	
    33	
    34	def category_for_band(band):
    35	    c = band[len("full_"):] if band.startswith("full_") else band
    36	    if c == "Flux":
    37	        return "Flux"
    38	    if c == "__Normalization_flat":
    39	        return "Normalization"
    40	    if c.startswith("Fr") or c.startswith("MFP_") or c.startswith("GEANT_"):
    41	        return "Hadronic response"
    42	    if (c.startswith("Muon_") or c.startswith("BeamAngle") or
    43	            c in {"MuonResolution", "MinosEfficiency"}):
    44	        return "Muon reconstruction"
    45	    return "Models"
    46	
    47	
    48	def load_flat(path):
    49	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    50	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    51	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] cannot open {path}")
    52	    h = f.Get("hXSecND_flat")
    53	    if not h:
    54	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] hXSecND_flat missing in {path}")
    55	    a = np.array([h.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(h.GetNbinsX())])
    56	    f.Close()
    57	    return a
    58	
    59	
    60	def main():
    61	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    62	    ap.add_argument("--cv", required=True)
    63	    ap.add_argument("--glob", required=True)
    64	    ap.add_argument("--add-norm", type=float, default=0.0)
    65	    ap.add_argument("--bootstrap-cov", nargs="+", default=None, metavar="ROOT:HIST")
    66	    ap.add_argument("--outdir", default="uq_5d/universe_stage2_5d")
    67	    ap.add_argument("--out-root", default="uq_universe_5d_covariance_combined.root")
    68	    args = ap.parse_args()
    69	    os.makedirs(args.outdir, exist_ok=True)
    70	
    71	    cv = load_flat(args.cv)
    72	    print(f"[INFO] CV flat nbins={cv.size}")
    73	    paths = sorted(glob.glob(args.glob))
    74	    if not paths:
    75	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] no universe files matched {args.glob}")
    76	
    77	    by_band = defaultdict(list)
    78	    for p in paths:
    79	        m = UNI_RE.match(os.path.basename(p))
    80	        if not m:
    81	            print(f"  [skip] {p}")
    82	            continue
    83	        by_band[m.group("band")].append((int(m.group("idx")), load_flat(p) - cv))
    84	    print(f"[INFO] {len(paths)} universe files across {len(by_band)} bands")
    85	
    86	    rep = cv > 0
    87	    n_rep = int(rep.sum())
    88	    print(f"[INFO] reported bins = {n_rep} of {cv.size}")
    89	    cv_rep = cv[rep]
    90	
    91	    band_cov, total = {}, np.zeros((n_rep, n_rep))
    92	    for band, entries in sorted(by_band.items()):
    93	        D = np.stack([d[rep] for _, d in sorted(entries)], axis=0)
    94	        if D.shape[0] < 2:
    95	            print(f"  [skip] {band}: {D.shape[0]} universe(s)")
    96	            continue
    97	        Z = D - D.mean(axis=0, keepdims=True)
    98	        cov = (Z.T @ Z) / D.shape[0]
    99	        band_cov[band] = cov
   100	        total += cov
   101	        diag = np.sqrt(np.maximum(np.diag(cov), 0))
   102	        with np.errstate(divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
   103	            rel = np.where(cv_rep > 0, diag / cv_rep, 0)
   104	        print(f"  [{band:24s} N={D.shape[0]:3d}] sqrt-tr={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(cov),0)):.3e} "
   105	              f"med rel={100*np.median(rel):.2f}%")
   106	
   107	    if args.add_norm > 0:
   108	        v = args.add_norm * cv_rep
   109	        nc = np.outer(v, v)
   110	        band_cov["__Normalization_flat"] = nc
   111	        total += nc
   112	        print(f"  [__Normalization_flat sigma={args.add_norm}] sqrt-tr={np.sqrt(np.trace(nc)):.3e}")
   113	
   114	    def cond_rank(m, rc=1e-12):
   115	        ev = np.linalg.eigvalsh(0.5 * (m + m.T))
   116	        pos = ev[ev > ev.max() * rc] if ev.size else ev
   117	        return int(pos.size)
   118	
   119	    tdiag = np.sqrt(np.maximum(np.diag(total), 0))
   120	    with np.errstate(divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
   121	        trel = np.where(cv_rep > 0, tdiag / cv_rep, 0)
   122	    print(f"\n[TOTAL syst 5D] sqrt-trace={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(total),0)):.3e} "
   123	          f"rank={cond_rank(total)}/{n_rep} median rel={100*np.median(trel):.2f}% "
   124	          f"p84={100*np.percentile(trel,84):.2f}%")
   125	
   126	    combined = None
   127	    if args.bootstrap_cov:
   128	        combined = total.copy()
   129	        for spec in args.bootstrap_cov:
   130	            bp, _, bh = spec.partition(":")
   131	            bh = bh or "hCov_stat_reported"
   132	            bf = ROOT.TFile.Open(bp)
   133	            hh = bf.Get(bh)
   134	            nb = hh.GetNbinsX()
   135	            extra = np.array([[hh.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1) for j in range(nb)]
   136	                              for i in range(nb)])
   137	            bf.Close()
   138	            if extra.shape != total.shape:
   139	                raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] {spec} shape {extra.shape} != {total.shape}")
   140	            combined += extra
   141	            print(f"  [+ {bp}:{bh} sqrt-tr={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(extra),0)):.3e}]")
   142	        cdiag = np.sqrt(np.maximum(np.diag(combined), 0))
   143	        with np.errstate(divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
   144	            crel = np.where(cv_rep > 0, cdiag / cv_rep, 0)
   145	        print(f"[COMBINED 5D] sqrt-trace={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(combined),0)):.3e} "
   146	              f"rank={cond_rank(combined)}/{n_rep} median rel={100*np.median(crel):.2f}%")
   147	
   148	    out = os.path.join(args.outdir, args.out_root)
   149	    rf = ROOT.TFile.Open(out, "RECREATE")
   150	
   151	    def wcov(name, mat):
   152	        n = mat.shape[0]
   153	        h = ROOT.TH2D(name, name, n, 0, n, n, 0, n)
   154	        for i in range(n):
   155	            for j in range(n):
   156	                h.SetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1, float(mat[i, j]))
   157	        h.Write()
   158	
   159	    wcov("hCov_universe5d_total", total)
   160	    for b, c in band_cov.items():
   161	        wcov(f"hCov_universe5d_{b}", c)
   162	    if combined is not None:
   163	        wcov("hCov_combined5d_total", combined)
   164	    rf.Close()
   165	    print(f"[wrote] {out}")
   166	
   167	    with open(os.path.join(args.outdir, "uq_universe_5d_summary.txt"), "w") as fh:
   168	        fh.write(f"CV: {args.cv}\nglob: {args.glob}\nreported bins: {n_rep}/{cv.size}\n")
   169	        fh.write(f"total syst sqrt-trace={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(total),0)):.4e} "
   170	                 f"median rel={100*np.median(trel):.3f}%\n")
   171	        if combined is not None:
   172	            fh.write(f"combined sqrt-trace={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(combined),0)):.4e} "
   173	                     f"median rel={100*np.median(crel):.3f}%\n")
   174	        grp = defaultdict(float)
   175	        for b, c in band_cov.items():
   176	            grp[category_for_band(b)] += np.sqrt(max(np.trace(c), 0))
   177	        for cat in CATEGORY_ORDER:
   178	            if grp[cat] > 0:
   179	                fh.write(f"  {cat:22s} sum sqrt-trace={grp[cat]:.3e}\n")
   180	    print(f"[wrote] {os.path.join(args.outdir, 'uq_universe_5d_summary.txt')}")
   181	
   182	
   183	if __name__ == "__main__":
   184	    main()

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""P4 validation: the selection-complete active scalar lateral covariance vs the
     3	support-limited dump-all-bank block. Matrix gates (symmetry / PSD / finite diag),
     4	sqrt-trace + per-bin comparison, and migration accounting from the active endpoints'
     5	census. Writes a compact JSON summary. Exit 0 iff all hard gates pass.
     6	
     7	Usage:
     8	  p4_validate_active_lateral.py \
     9	    --active uq_5d/active_lateral_stage2/active_scalar_lateral_5d_cov.root:hCov_universe5d_total \
    10	    --support uq_5d/universe_stage2_5d/uq_universe_5d_covariance_combined.root \
    11	    --merged-dir active_universe_5d/standard/merged \
    12	    --out uq_5d/active_lateral_stage2/p4_active_lateral_summary.json
    13	"""
    14	import argparse, json, os, sys
    15	import numpy as np
    16	import ROOT
    17	
    18	BANDS = ["BeamAngleX", "BeamAngleY", "MuonResolution",
    19	         "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    20	
    21	def load_th2(spec):
    22	    path, key = spec.rsplit(":", 1)
    23	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    24	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    25	        raise SystemExit(f"cannot open {path}")
    26	    h = f.Get(key)
    27	    if not h:
    28	        raise SystemExit(f"missing key {key} in {path}")
    29	    n = h.GetNbinsX()
    30	    C = np.empty((n, n))
    31	    for i in range(n):
    32	        for j in range(n):
    33	            C[i, j] = h.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1)
    34	    f.Close()
    35	    return C
    36	
    37	def load_th2_key(path, key):
    38	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    39	    h = f.Get(key)
    40	    if not h:
    41	        f.Close(); return None
    42	    n = h.GetNbinsX()
    43	    C = np.empty((n, n))
    44	    for i in range(n):
    45	        for j in range(n):
    46	            C[i, j] = h.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1)
    47	    f.Close()
    48	    return C
    49	
    50	def mat_gates(C, tag):
    51	    r = {}
    52	    r["shape"] = list(C.shape)
    53	    r["all_finite"] = bool(np.all(np.isfinite(C)))
    54	    asym = np.max(np.abs(C - C.T)) / max(1e-300, np.max(np.abs(C)))
    55	    r["rel_asymmetry"] = float(asym)
    56	    Cs = 0.5 * (C + C.T)
    57	    ev = np.linalg.eigvalsh(Cs)
    58	    r["min_eig"] = float(ev[0]); r["max_eig"] = float(ev[-1])
    59	    r["min_over_max_eig"] = float(ev[0] / max(1e-300, ev[-1]))
    60	    r["psd"] = bool(ev[0] >= -1e-12 * abs(ev[-1]))
    61	    d = np.diag(C)
    62	    r["diag_finite_nonneg"] = bool(np.all(np.isfinite(d)) and np.all(d >= -1e-30))
    63	    r["sqrt_trace"] = float(np.sqrt(np.clip(np.trace(Cs), 0, None)))
    64	    r["n_reported"] = int(np.sum(d > 0))
    65	    print(f"[{tag}] shape={r['shape']} finite={r['all_finite']} "
    66	          f"asym={asym:.2e} min/max_eig={r['min_over_max_eig']:.2e} PSD={r['psd']} "
    67	          f"sqrt_tr={r['sqrt_trace']:.4e}")
    68	    return r
    69	
    70	def main():
    71	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    72	    ap.add_argument("--active", required=True, help="ROOT:key of the active lateral total cov")
    73	    ap.add_argument("--support", required=True, help="uq_universe_5d_covariance_combined.root")
    74	    ap.add_argument("--merged-dir", default=None)
    75	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True)
    76	    a = ap.parse_args()
    77	
    78	    out = {"gates": {}, "active": {}, "support": {}, "comparison": {}, "migration": {}}
    79	    fails = []
    80	
    81	    Cact = load_th2(a.active)
    82	    out["active"] = mat_gates(Cact, "active")
    83	
    84	    # hard gates on the active (P4 product)
    85	    for g in ("all_finite", "psd", "diag_finite_nonneg"):
    86	        if not out["active"][g]:
    87	            fails.append(f"active {g}")
    88	    if out["active"]["rel_asymmetry"] > 1e-9:
    89	        fails.append("active asymmetry >1e-9")
    90	
    91	    # support-limited block = sum of the 5 kinematic per-band covs in the old combined ROOT
    92	    Csup = None; present = []
    93	    for b in BANDS:
    94	        cb = load_th2_key(a.support, f"hCov_universe5d_{b}")
    95	        if cb is None:
    96	            print(f"[support] band {b} key absent"); continue
    97	        present.append(b)
    98	        Csup = cb if Csup is None else Csup + cb
    99	    out["support"]["bands_present"] = present
   100	    if Csup is not None:
   101	        out["support"].update(mat_gates(Csup, "support(sum of 5 bands)"))
   102	        if Csup.shape == Cact.shape:
   103	            st_a = out["active"]["sqrt_trace"]; st_s = out["support"]["sqrt_trace"]
   104	            da = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(Cact), 0, None))
   105	            ds = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(Csup), 0, None))
   106	            m = ds > 0
   107	            ratio = np.divide(da, ds, out=np.zeros_like(da), where=m)
   108	            out["comparison"] = {
   109	                "sqrt_trace_active": st_a,
   110	                "sqrt_trace_support": st_s,
   111	                "sqrt_trace_ratio_active_over_support": float(st_a / max(1e-300, st_s)),
   112	                "per_bin_sigma_ratio_median": float(np.median(ratio[m])),
   113	                "per_bin_sigma_ratio_p16": float(np.percentile(ratio[m], 16)),
   114	                "per_bin_sigma_ratio_p84": float(np.percentile(ratio[m], 84)),
   115	                "n_bins_active_gt_support": int(np.sum(da > ds)),
   116	                "n_bins_common": int(np.sum(m)),
   117	            }
   118	            print(f"[compare] sqrt_tr active={st_a:.4e} support={st_s:.4e} "
   119	                  f"ratio={st_a/max(1e-300,st_s):.3f}; per-bin median ratio "
   120	                  f"{out['comparison']['per_bin_sigma_ratio_median']:.3f}")
   121	        else:
   122	            fails.append(f"active/support shape mismatch {Cact.shape} vs {Csup.shape}")
   123	
   124	    # migration accounting from the merged endpoint census (hadd sums TParameter<long>)
   125	    if a.merged_dir:
   126	        for b in BANDS:
   127	            per = {}
   128	            for ep in (0, 1):
   129	                p = os.path.join(a.merged_dir,
   130	                                 f"runEventLoopOmniFold_5D_MEFHC_active_{b}_{ep}.root")
   131	                if not os.path.exists(p):
   132	                    continue
   133	                f = ROOT.TFile.Open(p)
   134	                d = {}
   135	                for k in ("activeUniverseTruthEntrants", "activeUniverseTruthExits",
   136	                          "activeUniverseRecoEntrants", "activeUniverseRecoExits"):
   137	                    o = f.Get(k); d[k] = int(o.GetVal()) if o else None
   138	                f.Close()
   139	                per[f"idx{ep}"] = d
   140	            out["migration"][b] = per
   141	        # sanity: lateral bands should show nonzero total migration
   142	        tot = 0
   143	        for b, per in out["migration"].items():
   144	            for ep, d in per.items():
   145	                tot += sum(abs(v) for v in d.values() if isinstance(v, int))
   146	        out["migration"]["_total_abs"] = tot
   147	        print(f"[migration] total |entrants|+|exits| across bands/endpoints = {tot}")
   148	        if out["migration"] and tot == 0:
   149	            print("[migration] WARN: zero total migration (unexpected for lateral bands)")
   150	
   151	    out["gates"]["fails"] = fails
   152	    out["gates"]["result"] = "PASS" if not fails else "FAIL"
   153	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(a.out), exist_ok=True)
   154	    with open(a.out, "w") as fh:
   155	        json.dump(out, fh, indent=2)
   156	    print(f"\nRESULT {out['gates']['result']}" + ("" if not fails else " :: " + "; ".join(fails)))
   157	    print(f"summary -> {a.out}")
   158	    sys.exit(0 if not fails else 1)
   159	
   160	if __name__ == "__main__":
   161	    main()
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""P3S/P3F acceptance audit + receipt: enumerate the 5x2x12 active-universe
     3	endpoint ROOTs for a mode, validate each against the acceptance checks, and emit
     4	a compact machine-readable summary + downstream file map for the commit gate.
     5	
     6	Per-file acceptance checks:
     7	  schema (4 trees present), finite POT, event counts, native-miss metadata,
     8	  ENDPOINT IDENTITY (metadata band/idx match the filename), truth-authoritative
     9	  completeness (signal_reco==truth_denom), and point-cloud branch presence
    10	  (signal part_gen_*+part_reco_*; background+data part_reco_*) since these ROOTs
    11	  are dumped with MNV101_DUMP_POINTCLOUD=1.
    12	
    13	Campaign provenance recorded: git HEAD (source commit), installed-binary md5+mtime,
    14	launcher path + git blob hash (manifest hash proxy).
    15	
    16	Usage: p3s_manifest_summary.py [--mode standard|fps] [--out <json>]
    17	Exit 0 iff mode is 120/120 complete AND every present file passes; 2 otherwise.
    18	"""
    19	import argparse, hashlib, json, os, subprocess, sys
    20	import ROOT
    21	
    22	BANDS = ["BeamAngleX", "BeamAngleY", "MuonResolution",
    23	         "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    24	PLAYLISTS = ["1A","1B","1C","1D","1E","1F","1G","1L","1M","1N","1O","1P"]
    25	TREES = ["mc_truth_denom", "mc_signal_reco", "mc_background", "data"]
    26	SIG_PC = ["part_gen_E", "part_gen_pdg", "part_reco_E", "part_reco_pos", "part_reco_z"]
    27	RECO_PC = ["part_reco_E", "part_reco_pos", "part_reco_z"]
    28	
    29	def _sh(cmd):
    30	    try:
    31	        return subprocess.check_output(cmd, shell=True, text=True,
    32	                                       stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL).strip()
    33	    except Exception:
    34	        return None
    35	
    36	def get_val(f, name):
    37	    o = f.Get(name)
    38	    if o is None:
    39	        return None
    40	    if hasattr(o, "GetVal"):
    41	        return o.GetVal()
    42	    if hasattr(o, "GetTitle"):
    43	        return o.GetTitle()
    44	    return None
    45	
    46	def main():
    47	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    48	    ap.add_argument("--mode", default="standard")
    49	    ap.add_argument("--repo", default="/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold")
    50	    ap.add_argument("--out", default=None)
    51	    a = ap.parse_args()
    52	    base = os.path.join(a.repo, "nd-unfolding", "active_universe_5d", a.mode)
    53	    out = a.out or os.path.join(base, f"p3s_{a.mode}_manifest.json")
    54	    os.makedirs(base, exist_ok=True)
    55	
    56	    binpath = os.path.join(a.repo, "MINERvA101/opt/bin/runEventLoopOmniFold")
    57	    launcher = "nd-unfolding/sbatch_evloop_array_5d_active_laterals.sh"
    58	    prov = {
    59	        "mode": a.mode,
    60	        "source_commit": _sh(f"cd {a.repo} && git rev-parse HEAD"),
    61	        "binary_md5": _sh(f"md5sum {binpath} | cut -d' ' -f1"),
    62	        "binary_mtime": _sh(f"stat -c '%y' {binpath}"),
    63	        "launcher": launcher,
    64	        "launcher_git_blob": _sh(f"cd {a.repo} && git hash-object {launcher}"),
    65	        "expected_full_phase_space": (a.mode == "fps"),
    66	    }
    67	
    68	    files, present, missing, bad = [], 0, [], []
    69	    endpoints = {}
    70	    for band in BANDS:
    71	        for ep in (0, 1):
    72	            key = f"{band}_{ep}"
    73	            agg = {"playlists_present": 0, "size_bytes": 0,
    74	                   "entries": {t: 0 for t in TREES},
    75	                   "mcPOT": 0.0, "dataPOT": 0.0, "nMisses": 0,
    76	                   "migration_abs": 0, "isLateral": None, "identity_ok": 0}
    77	            for pl in PLAYLISTS:
    78	                p = os.path.join(base, key,
    79	                                 f"runEventLoopOmniFold_5D_{pl}_active_{band}_{ep}.root")
    80	                rec = {"band": band, "endpoint": ep, "playlist": pl,
    81	                       "path": os.path.relpath(p, a.repo),
    82	                       "exists": os.path.exists(p) and os.path.getsize(p) > 0}
    83	                if not rec["exists"]:
    84	                    missing.append(f"{key}/{pl}"); files.append(rec); continue
    85	                rec["size"] = os.path.getsize(p)
    86	                f = ROOT.TFile.Open(p)
    87	                if not f or f.IsZombie():
    88	                    rec["readable"] = False; bad.append(f"{key}/{pl}:zombie")
    89	                    files.append(rec); continue
    90	                rec["readable"] = True
    91	                checks = {}
    92	                # schema
    93	                ent = {}
    94	                for t in TREES:
    95	                    tr = f.Get(t); ent[t] = int(tr.GetEntries()) if tr else -1
    96	                rec["entries"] = ent
    97	                checks["schema_4trees"] = all(f.Get(t) for t in TREES)
    98	                checks["counts_positive"] = ent["mc_truth_denom"] > 0 and ent["mc_signal_reco"] > 0
    99	                # POT finite
   100	                mcPOT = get_val(f, "mcPOTUsed"); dataPOT = get_val(f, "dataPOTUsed")
   101	                import math
   102	                checks["pot_finite"] = (mcPOT is not None and dataPOT is not None
   103	                                        and math.isfinite(mcPOT) and math.isfinite(dataPOT)
   104	                                        and mcPOT > 0 and dataPOT > 0)
   105	                # native-miss metadata
   106	                nMiss = get_val(f, "nTruthOnlyMisses"); hasMiss = get_val(f, "hasTruthOnlyMisses")
   107	                checks["misses_meta"] = (nMiss is not None and hasMiss is not None)
   108	                # endpoint identity
   109	                aBand = get_val(f, "activeUniverseBand"); aIdx = get_val(f, "activeUniverseIndex")
   110	                aEn = get_val(f, "hasActiveUniverse"); aLat = get_val(f, "activeUniverseIsLateral")
   111	                checks["identity"] = (aBand == band and aIdx is not None and int(aIdx) == ep
   112	                                      and aEn == 1 and aLat == 1)
   113	                rec["identity"] = {"band": aBand, "idx": aIdx, "hasActive": aEn, "isLateral": aLat}
   114	                # completeness (Phase 18.2)
   115	                td, sg = ent["mc_truth_denom"], ent["mc_signal_reco"]
   116	                comp = (sg / td) if td else None
   117	                rec["completeness"] = comp
   118	                checks["completeness"] = (comp is not None and abs(comp - 1.0) < 0.02)
   119	                # point-cloud presence
   120	                sigtr, bkgtr, datr = f.Get("mc_signal_reco"), f.Get("mc_background"), f.Get("data")
   121	                checks["pc_signal"] = all(sigtr and sigtr.GetBranch(b) for b in SIG_PC)
   122	                checks["pc_bkg"] = all(bkgtr and bkgtr.GetBranch(b) for b in RECO_PC)
   123	                checks["pc_data"] = all(datr and datr.GetBranch(b) for b in RECO_PC)
   124	                f.Close()
   125	                rec["checks"] = checks
   126	                rec["pass"] = all(checks.values())
   127	                mig = 0
   128	                files.append(rec)
   129	                if rec["pass"]:
   130	                    present += 1
   131	                    agg["playlists_present"] += 1
   132	                    agg["size_bytes"] += rec["size"]
   133	                    for t in TREES:
   134	                        if ent[t] > 0: agg["entries"][t] += ent[t]
   135	                    agg["mcPOT"] += mcPOT or 0.0; agg["dataPOT"] += dataPOT or 0.0
   136	                    agg["nMisses"] += int(nMiss or 0)
   137	                    if aLat is not None: agg["isLateral"] = int(aLat)
   138	                    agg["identity_ok"] += 1
   139	                else:
   140	                    bad.append(f"{key}/{pl}:" + ",".join(k for k, v in checks.items() if not v))
   141	            td, sg = agg["entries"]["mc_truth_denom"], agg["entries"]["mc_signal_reco"]
   142	            agg["completeness_sig_over_td"] = (sg / td) if td else None
   143	            endpoints[key] = agg
   144	
   145	    summary = {"provenance": prov, "expected": 120, "present_and_passing": present,
   146	               "complete": present == 120, "missing": missing, "failing": bad,
   147	               "endpoints": endpoints}
   148	    with open(out, "w") as fh:
   149	        json.dump({"summary": summary, "files": files}, fh, indent=2)
   150	
   151	    print(f"[audit {a.mode}] passing={present}/120 complete={summary['complete']} "
   152	          f"missing={len(missing)} failing={len(bad)}")
   153	    print(f"[prov] commit={prov['source_commit'][:10] if prov['source_commit'] else '?'} "
   154	          f"bin_md5={prov['binary_md5']} launcher_blob={ (prov['launcher_git_blob'] or '?')[:10]}")
   155	    for key, agg in endpoints.items():
   156	        c = agg["completeness_sig_over_td"]
   157	        print(f"  {key:26s} {agg['playlists_present']:2d}/12  lat={agg['isLateral']}  "
   158	              f"id_ok={agg['identity_ok']}  c={('%.6f'%c) if c else 'NA':>8}  misses={agg['nMisses']}")
   159	    if bad:
   160	        print("FAILING:", "; ".join(bad[:12]))
   161	    print(f"summary -> {out}")
   162	    sys.exit(0 if summary["complete"] and not bad else 2)
   163	
   164	if __name__ == "__main__":
   165	    main()

     1	# P2 — #16 active-universe event-loop interface validation
     2	
     3	Validation record for the `MNV101_ACTIVE_UNIVERSE=BAND:IDX` selection-complete
     4	event-loop mode. This is the gate before any P3 active-universe production family
     5	is launched. A smoke pass is **not** evidence that any production endpoint exists.
     6	
     7	- **Date:** 2026-07-15 (UTC 12:49–12:59)
     8	- **Source commit gated with this record.** Prior HEAD `01660fa`.
     9	- **Binary (rebuilt + installed from current source):**
    10	  `MINERvA101/opt/bin/runEventLoopOmniFold`, md5 `e63c74961d699313ef155065fc790ff1`,
    11	  383448 bytes, 9 `ACTIVE_UNIVERSE` strings. Rebuilt via
    12	  `2d-unfolding/sbatch_build.sh` recipe (cmake `--target runEventLoopOmniFold`
    13	  + `cmake --install .`). Downstream P3 tasks re-print this md5 per task.
    14	- **Compute:** `gpu_interactive` salloc 55933725 (nid001017), CPU-only via
    15	  `srun --gres=none`; env `setup_salloc_env.sh` with `HOME=/global/homes/j/josephrb`.
    16	- **Smoke inputs:** `interface_smoke/1A_{MC,Data}_smoke.txt` (4 MC + 8 Data
    17	  files derived from the canonical 1A manifests). Smoke ROOTs are disposable
    18	  (`*.root` gitignored) and are **not** production inputs.
    19	- **Readback validator:** `interface_smoke/p2_validate.py`.
    20	
    21	## Interface source (owned by Agent A: `runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp`)
    22	Adds a selection-complete promotion mode distinct from the CV-support-limited
    23	`MNV101_DUMP_UNIVERSES` shadow-branch dump:
    24	- `ParseActiveUniverse(BAND:IDX)` with strict format/index/range parsing.
    25	- The requested `(band, idx)` reco+truth universe objects are promoted to the
    26	  ordinary event-loop universe, so truth/reco selection, kinematics, weights,
    27	  backgrounds, truth-authoritative IDs, bilateral dedupe, and native misses are
    28	  all rebuilt in that universe.
    29	- A per-event migration census vs CV (unique-key, counted once regardless of
    30	  selection) is written as `TParameter<long>`
    31	  `activeUniverse{Truth,Reco}{Entrants,Exits}`.
    32	- Metadata: `TNamed activeUniverseBand`, `TParameter<int>`
    33	  `activeUniverseIndex`, `hasActiveUniverse`, `activeUniverseIsLateral`.
    34	- `MNV101_ACTIVE_UNIVERSE` is rejected with `MNV101_SKIP_SYST` (universe maps
    35	  would not be built). CV-mode output is preserved bit-for-identical: the
    36	  `if(!isSignalTruth) continue;` is only relocated past the reco computation so
    37	  the census sees all events; the signal-reco tree still fills signal-truth rows
    38	  only, and the CV comparison universe is null when inactive.
    39	- `MNV101_DUMP_POINTCLOUD=1` now also dumps `part_reco_{E,pos,z}` on the
    40	  background tree (mirrors data/signal), so P5 shifted-cloud processing has
    41	  selection-complete background clouds.
